topic: How to check your boiler pressure and top it up safely
facebook:
📉 Boiler pressure dropped below 1 bar? Here's how to top it up yourself:

1️⃣ Find the filling loop — usually a silver braided hose under the boiler
2️⃣ Open the valves slowly until the gauge reads 1.5 bar
3️⃣ Close the valves and restart the boiler

If it drops again within a few weeks, that's a sign of a leak or internal fault — worth getting checked before it becomes a bigger problem.

https://bettercallwes.co.uk/services/boiler-repair.html
Call/WhatsApp Wes: 07700 155 655
instagram:
Boiler pressure dropped? Here's how to fix it yourself 📉

Step 1: Find the filling loop — usually a silver braided hose underneath the boiler
Step 2: Open both valves slowly until the gauge reads 1.5 bar
Step 3: Close the valves and restart the boiler

If it keeps dropping every few weeks, that's not normal — it usually points to a small leak or internal fault worth getting looked at.

🔗 Link in bio

topic: Repair vs replace — the honest guide for homeowners
facebook:
🤔 Should you repair your boiler or replace it?

Here's the honest answer — not the one that makes me the most money:

✅ Repair if: the boiler is under 10 years old, fault is minor, and repair cost is under half a new boiler
🔄 Replace if: it's over 15 years old, repairs are frequent, or efficiency is costing you on bills

I'll always tell you which option makes sense for your situation before I start any work.

https://bettercallwes.co.uk/services/boiler-repair.html
Call/WhatsApp Wes: 07700 155 655
instagram:

✅ It's under 10 years old
✅ The fault is straightforward

Replace if:

facebook:

2️⃣ Look for an error code on the display and note it down
3️⃣ Send me a WhatsApp message with a video or photo of the boiler

I'll get back to you as quickly as I can and often diagnose the issue remotely so we're ready to fix it first visit.

topic: Cold radiators — how to bleed them yourself
facebook:
🌡️ Got a radiator that's hot at the bottom but cold at the top? It needs bleeding.

Here's how:

1️⃣ Turn the heating off and let the radiator cool
2️⃣ Use a radiator key (or flat-head screwdriver) on the bleed valve — usually top right
3️⃣ Turn slowly anticlockwise until you hear hissing air
4️⃣ When water starts to drip, close the valve
5️⃣ Check your boiler pressure afterwards — top up if needed

If the radiator is cold all over, or the problem keeps coming back, that's a different issue worth looking into.

https://bettercallwes.co.uk/services/radiators.html
Call/WhatsApp Wes: 07700 155 655
instagram:
Radiator hot at the bottom, cold at the top? Time to bleed it 🌡️

How to bleed your radiator:

1️⃣ Heat off — let it cool first
2️⃣ Find the bleed valve (top corner) — use a radiator key
3️⃣ Turn anticlockwise until you hear hissing air escaping
4️⃣ When water appears, close the valve
5️⃣ Check boiler pressure — top up if it's dropped

If it keeps happening or the radiator is cold all over, message me — could be a different problem.

🔗 Link in bio

A radiator that's hot at the bottom but cold at the top usually just needs bleeding to release trapped air.

Turn the heating off and let it cool, use a radiator key on the bleed valve (usually top right), turn anticlockwise until air hisses out, then close when water appears. Check your boiler pressure and top up if needed.

If the radiator stays cold all over or the problem recurs, it may point to sludge build-up or another fault worth investigating.

✅ All gas appliances inspected (boiler, hob, fire)
✅ Flues and ventilation checked
✅ Gas pressure and flow tested
✅ Safety devices verified
✅ CP12 certificate issued the same day

It's a legal requirement every 12 months for rental properties with gas appliances. The certificate needs to be given to tenants within 28 days.

topic: Suspected gas leak — what to do right now
facebook:
⚠️ If you smell gas at home, act immediately:

🚫 Don't switch any lights or electrics on or off
🚫 Don't use your phone inside the property
✅ Open doors and windows
✅ Turn off the gas at the mains (the lever by your meter — turn it 90°)
✅ Get out of the property
✅ Call the National Gas Emergency Service: 0800 111 999 (free, 24/7)

Once it's been declared safe by the emergency service, I can come out to find and fix the source.

https://bettercallwes.co.uk/services/gas-leak-detection.html
📲 07700 155 655

topic: Power flushing — when your heating system needs it
facebook:
🔧 3 signs your heating system needs a power flush:

1️⃣ Radiators take a long time to heat up or have cold patches
2️⃣ Your boiler is making rumbling or kettling noises
3️⃣ The water from your radiator bleed valve is very dark or discoloured

Sludge and rust build up in older systems and reduce efficiency. A power flush removes it and can significantly extend the life of your boiler.

https://bettercallwes.co.uk/services/power-flushing.html
Call/WhatsApp Wes: 07700 155 655

topic: Smart heating controls — are they worth it?
facebook:
📱 Are smart heating controls actually worth fitting?

Honestly? For most people, yes — but it depends how you use them.

✅ You can heat your home only when you're in it
✅ Control from your phone — useful if your schedule changes
✅ Some systems learn your routine and adjust automatically

The savings vary depending on your current setup and habits. But if you're still on a basic programmer, the difference can be noticeable on your bills over a year.

I fit and set them up properly — not just clip one on and leave.

topic: Shower losing pressure — common causes and what to check
facebook:
🚿 Shower pressure suddenly dropped? Here's what's usually behind it:

1️⃣ Blocked shower head — limescale build-up reduces flow over time
2️⃣ Faulty shower cartridge or valve
3️⃣ Drop in overall mains pressure (check taps elsewhere)
4️⃣ Partially closed isolation valve under or behind the shower

For a blocked shower head, try soaking it in white vinegar overnight. If that doesn't fix it — or it's a valve or cartridge issue — give me a call.

https://bettercallwes.co.uk/services/shower-repair.html

topic: Combi vs system boiler — which is right for your home?
facebook:
🔧 Combi boiler vs system boiler — which is right for your home?

Combi: Heats water on demand, no hot water cylinder needed. Great for smaller homes and flats with 1-2 bathrooms.

System: Works with a hot water cylinder, better for homes with multiple bathrooms where several people are using hot water at the same time.

Getting this wrong means either running out of hot water or paying for a system bigger than you need. I'll always recommend the right fit for your home before quoting.

https://bettercallwes.co.uk/services/combi-boiler-installations.html
Call/WhatsApp Wes: 07700 155 655
instagram:
Combi or system boiler — which do you actually need? 🔧

Combi: On-demand hot water, no cylinder. Best for smaller homes and 1-2 bathrooms.

System: Works with a hot water tank. Better for larger homes with multiple bathrooms in use at the same time.

Getting this wrong means you're either constantly waiting for hot water or overpaying for capacity you don't need.

I'll tell you the right option for your home before quoting — not the most expensive one.

Choosing between a combi and system boiler is one of the first decisions in any new boiler installation — and getting it right matters.

A combi boiler heats water on demand with no storage cylinder, making it ideal for smaller homes and properties with one or two bathrooms. A system boiler works with a hot water cylinder and suits larger homes where multiple bathrooms are used simultaneously.

I'll recommend the right option for your home before providing a quote.
